title: Neottia longicaulis
uses: Neottia longicaulis is traditionally used in some cultures for its potential medicinal properties, although the available information on this specific species is limited. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may be beneficial in treating various health conditions. In traditional Chinese medicine, Neottia longicaulis is used to treat fever, headache, and other health issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Neottia longicaulis as a medicinal plant. taxonomy: Neottia longicaulis is a species of orchid native to North America. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Neottia longicaulis is further classified into the genus Neottia, which contains about 50 species of terrestrial orchids. The genus Neottia is part of the subfamily Neottioideae, which is a subfamily within the family Orchidaceae. Neottia longicaulis is also sometimes classified under the synonym Listera longicaulis.

intro: Neottia longicaulis, also known as the white lady's tresses orchid, is a perennial flowering plant native to Asia and North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neottia longicaulis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neottia longicaulis include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
